title: Prove key properties with formal verification
description: Use formal property checking on control logic and protocol rules where exhaustive proof beats random sampling.
body: Use when a property must hold for all inputs, such as a protocol rule or safety invariant. Do not rely on random simulation for properties that demand exhaustiveness, since simulation samples rather than proves. Actions: write the property as an assertion, run a formal engine to prove it, and inspect any counterexample. Validation signals include a proven property or a concrete counterexample trace and bounded proofs reaching a useful depth. Risk boundary: an inconclusive proof is not a pass, so escalate to constraints or abstraction rather than claiming a property holds when the engine could not prove it.
